title: WHATWG URL Resolution, Identity, Unicode, and Scope Admission · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in conservative WHATWG URL identity pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. objectives: Parse URLs with WHATWG URL and inspect scheme, credentials, host, port, path, query, and fragment before trust decisions.

Remove fragments from fetch identity while preserving document-location fragments when findings need them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 7701 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fetch identity, conservative keys, IDNA, and rejected schemes; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep display and network host forms separate, reject credentials and unsafe schemes, and expose failed or confusable IDNA decisions.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: WHATWG URL Living Standard — Controls parsing, resolution, serialization, origin, host, IDNA, and URL state.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Idna Display Boundary · fault isolation · Debug Idna Displa…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Idna Display Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Idna Display Boundary · fault isolation · Debug Idna Displa…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug TypeScript team handles case 3192 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fetch identity, conservative keys, IDNA, and rejected schemes; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep display and network host forms separate, reject credentials and unsafe schemes, and expose failed or confusable IDNA decisions.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Idna Display Boundary · fault isolation · Debug Idna Displa…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Unicode and ASCII host forms can be interchanged without validation.

stimulus.title: Idna Display Boundary failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Unicode and ASCII host forms can be interchanged without validation.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep display and network host forms separate, reject credentials and unsafe schemes, and expose failed or confusable IDNA decisions.

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Idna Display Boundary.
Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the idna display boundary · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For idna display boundary · fault isolation, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Idna Display Bound…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-idna-display-boundary-10xbtc" that uses case 2035 to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fetch identity, conservative keys, IDNA, and rejected schemes. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Idna Display Bound….
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Idna Display Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Idna Display Bound…, keep Idna Display Boundary connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep display and network host forms separate, reject credentials and unsafe schemes, and expose failed or confusable IDNA decisions.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-idna-display-boundary-10xbtc(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_idna_display_boundary_10xbtc\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?urlCases < 14)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stableKeys < 9 \|\| stableKeys > urlCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?boundaryCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafeMerges > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
